meta.name: target_lanes
meta.purpose: Normalized lane map for fit scoring and resume-direction decisions.

whyItFits: Best-aligned title in the current priority stack.
Matches process ownership, customer coordination, documentation, and cross-functional execution evidence.

primaryLanes.displayName: Sales Operations Coordinator / Sales Ops Analyst (entry or support level)
sourceTitles: Sales Operations Analyst / Sales Ops Analyst
whyItFits: Strong fit with KPI design, Airtable/system thinking, follow-up workflows, documentation, and process governance.
Needs support-level framing rather than advanced analyst framing.
Strong thematic fit with onboarding, handoff reduction, process documentation, and cross-functional execution.
Should be framed as coordination and rollout support, not deep software implementation ownership.
Strong thematic alignment, but quantified proof and enterprise-tool depth remain weaker than the top lanes.

secondaryLanes.displayName: Enablement Analyst / Training or Enablement Coordinator
whyItFits: Supported by training/seminar design and systems-thinking, but employer-facing proof is narrower than operations lanes.

secondaryLanes.displayName: Customer Onboarding / Customer Success Operations Coordinator
sourceTitles: Customer Onboarding / Customer Success Operations-type roles
Client Onboarding / Customer Success Operations Coordinator
whyItFits: Directly supported by onboarding, CRM organization, and customer communication language.
Use cautiously if roles require deep SaaS retention metrics or account-management scale not supported in the record.
Supported by scheduling, coordination, documentation, and field-informed execution.
Good bridge lane when the role emphasizes organization over formal PM credentials.
Supported by client communication, bid support, commercial account coordination, and active customer management.

adjacentLanes.displayName: Marketing Operations Coordinator / CRM Lifecycle Marketing Coordinator
whyItFits: Supported by campaign planning, lead flow/process support, and CRM follow-up logic.

avoidUnless: quantitative proof is not central to the role
stretchOrCautionLanes.reason: Current record should not be inflated into deep enterprise analytics or advanced systems ownership.
